page: Amazon Web Services m5d.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor running at 3.1 GHz, delivering 64 dedicated vCPUs across 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes 2400 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes, and it supports a baseline network bandwidth of 20 Gbps. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

Benchmark data indicates a clear contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core CPU metrics, including stress-ng and Geekbench single-core scores, rank in the bottom 25%. However, multi-core performance scales effectively, with stress-ng multi-core, Passmark CPU, and Geekbench multi-core scores landing in the average middle 50% range. Notably, memory bandwidth for large blocks is strong, placing in the top 25% for both read and write operations, which is complemented by strong performance in Passmark CPU extended instruction tests.

Given its balanced resource allocation and high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage, the m5d.16xlarge is well-suited for workloads requiring a mix of compute, memory, and high-speed local storage. These include database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded data processing. While its single-core performance and LLM text generation speeds are weak, its high resource density and strong memory bandwidth offer qualitative cost efficiency for parallelized enterprise applications and local caching databases that do not require GPU acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services m5d.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL x86_64 processor at 3.1 GHz. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 2400 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor with a 20 Gbps baseline network speed. Performance benchmarks show weak single-core CPU metrics but average multi-core capabilities, alongside strong large-block memory bandwidth. Lacking a GPU, this instance is not suited for heavy LLM text generation but performs reliably in multi-threaded tasks. The combination of balanced compute-to-memory resources and high-speed local storage makes the m5d.16xlarge highly effective for database operations, static web serving, and general-purpose enterprise workloads requiring fast local data access.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m5d.16xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 64 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 2400 GB local NVMe SSD storage, optimized for databases and multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m5d.16xlarge features 64 vCPUs, 256 GB DDR4 RAM, and 2400 GB NVMe SSD storage for balanced general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring high-capacity local NVMe SSD storage and strong memory bandwidth for multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated vCPUs via Intel Xeon 8259CL processor at 3.1 GHz
256.0 GB of DDR4 memory with strong large-block read/write bandwidth
2400 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage
20 Gbps baseline network bandwidth powered by AWS Nitro hypervisor
Best suited for database operations, static web serving, and multi-threaded applications
